beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: Nine months of runway and a family in the queue means we need one paying customer before you touch that resignation letter.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: Nine months of savings with a family on the way doesn't mean starting a business is easy, it means your runway is actually four.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: Nine months of savings and zero kids is the most runway we will ever have; at thirty-four, waiting longer is just called surrender.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: You have exactly nine months of savings, a partner, and a family to plan, so we need verified health insurance costs before you leap.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old self, waiting is not surrender when nine months of savings instantly shrinks to four the moment we pay for a family's healthcare.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old me, shut up. At thirty-four, baby formula costs money, and "surrender" is just what you call paying rent on time.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: Mentor, if we wait for that first customer while playing it safe, we'll bleed our nine-month runway dry on hesitation, not execution.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old self, nine months evaporates instantly when you're paying for prenatal care and family health insurance out of pocket. Wake up.

beats.label: Verdict
beats.speaker: Council chair
beats.text: At thirty-four, with a family on the horizon, freedom is earned through calculated execution, not blind leaps of faith.

verdict.decision: Go full-time with your business, but only after securing your first paying customer.
verdict.conditions: You must land one paying client and secure a viable family healthcare plan before resigning.
verdict.firstMove: Draft a pitch email to three warm leads from your past side projects to secure that first client.
verdict.flipRisk: If your partner becomes pregnant before you secure a paying client, you must stay employed.

alignment.keyConcerns: Securing one paying customer before launching to protect the runway.
Underestimating the true, rapid burn rate of family expenses.
Allowing fear and endless planning to kill the dream entirely.
Losing stable health insurance during prenatal care and family planning.
